items.title: States and territories agree to extra fuel excise cut using GST revenue

children.content: Premiers and chief ministers agree to use an estimated $400 million in extra GST revenue to fund a 5.7 per litre cut to the fuel excise, on top of the Commonwealth's 26.3 cent per litre relief.

articles.title: WA invokes emergency powers to address fuel supply crisis

children.content: The Western Australian government will use emergency powers to force fuel companies to provide information about their supply chains in an effort to keep fuel flowing through the state.

image.altText: WA Premier Roger Cook speaks to reporters

articles.title: Perth's 'Cheap fuel Tuesdays' are off the table. Here's why

children.content: Perth motorists can no longer rely on "cheap Tuesday" fuel prices and are urged to shop around every day as the Middle East war disrupts global supply chains.

image.altText: Cars lining up at a petrol station.

items.title: Ongoing safety concerns delay reopening of WA tree climb

children.content: Plans to reopen the climb up the Gloucester Tree by the Easter long weekend have fallen by the wayside as work continues to make it safer for visitors.

image.altText: View down the tree from the top

items.title: Easter temperatures to drop in Perth as another Queensland cyclone looms

children.content: For most of the country, skies have cleared up just in time for Easter — but Western Australia perhaps has the most interesting weather ahead.

image.altText: A map of Australia with expected rain totals for the Easter long weekend, showing orange along the southern and eastern coasts.

items.title: Backpackers lose jobs in the wake of ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle

children.content: In a WA town long reliant on backpackers, there may be few workers left by the time it is ready to reopen for business.

items.title: Bargain avocados in high demand but growers pay price

children.content: A bumper avocado season is creating a perfect storm for cheaper produce and record export volumes, but growers are doing it tough.

image.altText: Close up of avocado in a tray.

items.title: WA man charged with terrorism offences wrote of Bondi attacks in diary, court told

children.content: Jayson Joseph Michaels, 20, had reflected on the Bondi attacks in his diary and was allegedly planning a "day of justice", a Perth court hears.

image.altText: A smiling youg man in high-vis in front of a digger holding a hose.

items.title: How kids are connecting to country in one of Australia's most isolated communities

children.content: Kalumburu youth workers are giving teens the chance to get away from screens, be on country and connect with nature.

items.title: Western Australia at risk of mouse plague 'spiralling out of control'

children.content: Grain paddocks in Western Australia are filled with thousands of mice which are making their way into residential areas.

gallery.altText: A mouse in a paddock with red dirt and crop stubble.

thumbnail.altText: A mouse in a paddock with red dirt and crop stubble.

items.title: 'Domino effect': Why Singapore's oil shock will play out in Australia

children.content: Singapore is exposed to the oil shock from Iran's effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz, and its response will shape the fuel crisis in Australia.

image.altText: Chimneys and white storage tankers next to a bay of grey water.

items.title: Track the latest petrol and diesel prices around Australia

children.content: See what's happening with petrol and diesel prices near you, updated constantly.

items.title: Powerful magnitude-7.4 earthquake strikes Indonesia, tsunami warning issued

children.content: The quake, which hit early today, had a depth of 35 kilometres and its epicentre was 127km west-north-west of Ternate, Indonesia, the US Geological Survey said.

image.altText: An aerial view of islands around Raja Ampat in east Indonesia's West Papua region

items.title: International crypto firm targets uni students despite ASIC ban

children.content: A major cryptocurrency platform is operating like "gambling" in Australia, according to experts, despite new regulatory laws.

image.altText: Two large gold coins bearing engraved "b"s lie on top of a complex financial line-and-bar graph

items.title: Marsh produces IPL fireworks but Rizvi guides Capitals to victory

children.content: Mitch Marsh launches his Indian Premier League season with three big sixes, but it was not enough to help the Lucknow Super Giants in a six-wicket loss to Delhi Capitals.

image.altText: Mitchell Marsh bats for Lucknow Super Giants in IPL match against Delhi Capitals.

items.title: A blood moon, a cyclone and rainbows — WA's skies, captured by you

children.content: Nature put on a special show for Western Australia in March, including a total lunar eclipse and rough seas from a powerful tropical cyclone, in addition to the spectacular sunsets the state is known for.

Rainbows are seen in the sky

items.title: Sit back and let us take you on a journey to the Moon with Artemis II

children.content: If all goes well for NASA's mission, the astronauts aboard could fly the furthest that humans have ever gone. What should we be looking out for?

image.altText: A 3D wireframe rocket with 3 male and 1 female astronauts pose with looming large moon behind

items.title: ASX unimpressed by Trump speech

children.content: ASX drops back following President Donald Trump's speech on Iran. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: A woman walks near an ASX stock exchange board.

items.title: Perth house prices surge as Australia's two biggest cities go backwards

children.content: There is a significant divide between property price growth in major cities as buyers weigh up interest rate hikes and geopolitical tensions.

image.altText: A drone view of houses in a suburb of Perth.

items.title: Bondi gunman fails to have family's identities suppressed for 40 years

children.content: Lawyers for Bondi gunman Naveed Akram fail in a bid to have the identities of his mother, brother and sister protected by a decades-long court suppression order on safety grounds.

image.altText: Naveed Akram

items.title: Locals say country city of 40,000 sorely lacking public transport services

children.content: Busselton has just three regular bus services, leaving 85-year-old Jan Whalan with an hour's trip on her mobility scooter to get to the shops — but as the fuel crisis also bites, there are calls for better public transport.

items.title: Here is the key to getting through The Super Mario Galaxy Movie

children.content: Even with downgraded expectations, the Nintendo sequel is little more than Easter eggs and bright colours. But that won't stop it from filling cinemas.

image.altText: A shot from Super Mario Galaxy Movie featuring Mario, Luigi, Peach and Yoshi

items.title: Museum fans: Test yourself with our curious collection of trivia

children.content: Australia’s museums hold a treasure trove of odd facts, native animals and even burlesque history. So how much do you know about the things hidden in our nation’s vaults?

image.altText: A close up of a plastic dinosaur.

items.title: WA young gun Connolly makes stunning IPL debut to seal thrilling win

children.content: Cooper Connolly puts in a game-winning performance for Punjab in his IPL debut.

image.altText: GettyImages-2252677170

items.title: 'No one deserves this': WA mum speaks out about vaccine decline

children.content: Naomi Steenson doesn't want to see other parents go through what she did with her son Blake, as new data shows 80,000 Australian children aged five and under are not fully up to date with their immunisations.

items.title: 'Dance for me': Australian Harrods survivor tells of Mohamed Al Fayed abuse

children.content: Jessica tried to keep her distance from Harrods billionaire owner Mohamed Al Fayed, who was known to abuse his female staff, until he spotted her going down an escalator.

image.altText: A woman looks directly at the camera.

items.title: One Nation candidate published antisemitic, racist and homophobic posts

children.content: A One Nation candidate in the South Australian election was accused of domestic abuse, bullying and harassment, while another made racist, antisemitic and homophobic social media posts, raising fresh questions about the party's vetting processes as it surges in popularity.

image.altText: A collage of screenshots of tweets on top of an image of Tyler Green smiling while posing for a photo.

items.title: Explaining Carlton's infamous second-half fade-outs

children.content: Carlton's horrible second halves have become a costly trend which has the team struggling and Michael Voss fighting for his job. This is what is going wrong after half-time for the Blues.

image.altText: Three Carlton players look puzzled while chatting on the field after a defeat

items.title: Australia wasting migrant talent on an 'industrial scale', experts say

children.content: Australia is failing to recognise the qualifications of highly trained permanent migrants, former Treasury secretary Martin Parkinson says.

image.altText: A worker wearing a protective helmet welds a girder.

items.title: Prized WA tourism hotspots forced to close to holiday-makers for Easter

children.content: Famed holiday hotspots in Western Australia's north, including Exmouth and Coral Bay, are reluctantly turning away tourists ahead of the Easter holiday peak as they rebuild in the wake of Cyclone Narelle.

articles.title: Damage to pastoral stations a 'kick in the guts' after cyclone

children.content: West Australian pastoralists have faced severe flooding after ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle wreaked havoc in one of the state's most important food-producing regions.

image.altText: An aerial view of flooded cattle station

thumbnail.altText: An aerial shot of a bright blue rugged coastline

items.title: 'Always assumed fuel would be available': Miner scales back as crisis bites

children.content: Junior West Australian iron ore miner Fenix Resources is forced to scale back non-essential activities to keep its operations running after coming within days of running out of fuel.

image.altText: Mining boss speaking at conference

items.title: The movement on the rise that police believe aided fugitive Dezi Freeman

children.content: As detectives investigate whether the sovereign citizen community helped harbour police killer Dezi Freeman, researchers say the anti-government movement appears to be surging in regional Australia.

image.altText: Man holding phone horizontally while wearing body camera on a property that looks like a farm with mountain in the background

items.title: 'Cameras watching': Horrifying trend of technology-based abuse

children.content: One in three women on temporary visas are experiencing domestic and family violence, safety advocates say.

image.altText: A professional with glasses and a sombre expression.

items.title: Man jailed for life for fatal stabbing of 'kind and loving' woman

children.content: Samuel Jade Lincoln Jingle will have to serve a minimum of 19 years in prison after killing his partner in Fitzroy Crossing.

image.altText: Exterior shot of the WA Supreme Court building in Perth with red kangaroo paws in the foreground at left.

items.title: Greyhound racing regulator defends controversial sport at inquiry

children.content: The body governing WA greyhound racing says it is determined to maintain the welfare of both dogs and the industry as it gives evidence at a parliamentary inquiry, sparked by a petition that garnered 26,000 signatures.

articles.title: 'Breaking point': Ex-greyhound racing insider speaks out as inquiry begins

children.content: A former greyhound racing insider tells how he reached "breaking point" with the industry, as a parliamentary inquiry into the sport in WA begins.
